data.title: Tootsie Roll Industries declares $0.09 dividend
data.description:
• Tootsie Roll Industries (NYSE:TR) declares $0.09/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Payable Jan. 9; for shareholders of record Dec. 18; ex-div Dec. 18.
  • See TR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P seek firm direction after hitting record highs; bitcoin climbs past $101K
data.description:
Wall Street's major market averages were mostly muted Thursday, a day after each closed at fresh record highs, while investors pushed the price of bitcoin (BTC-USD) beyond its newly crossed $100K milestone.

Technology stocks (XLY) as a whole were lower after that group paced the S&P 500's (SP500) gain a day earlier, contributing to the benchmark's 56th record closing high in 2024. UBS said even as the earnings gap narrows between Big Tech stocks and the rest of the market, it sees mega-cap techs playing a leading role in driving markets higher in 2025.

On Thursday, the S&P 500 (SP500) and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) wavered and were each down less than -0.1%. The Dow (DJI) -0.2%, after closing above 45,000 for the first time on Wednesday.

Tesla (TSLA) outperformed its fellow Magnificent 7 stocks on Thursday, rising +3.5% after Bank of America Securities raised its price target on the EV maker to $400 from $350.

Meanwhile, Tesla (TSLA) landed in Seeking Alpha's list of 20 companies that have the most bitcoin (BTC-USD) holdings, with the cryptocurrency in focus after crossing above $100K late Wednesday. Bitcoin (BTC-USD) on Thursday rose +7% to trade above $101,570.

"Bitcoin flows have been dominated by institutional investors in 2024. We expect this to continue in 2025, helping BTC reach our end-2025 target level of around $200,000," Geoff Kendrick, head of crypto research at Standard Chartered, said Thursday.

MicroStrategy (MSTR) is well-known as the corporate holder of the the most bitcoins, at 252,220. Its shares slipped 0.2% on Thursday, but the stock has soared +540% in 2024. Shares of bitcoin miner MARA (MARA) were down 1%.

In economic news, U.S. initial jobless claims increased more than expected in the past week. Meanwhile, outplacement firm Challenger, Gray & Christmas said announced job cuts rose 3.8% in November and climbed 27% Y/Y. The reports about the labor market came before Friday's arrival of U.S. November nonfarm payrolls data.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.20%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.17%.

"It’s been a very eventful 24 hours for markets, with the French government losing a no-confidence vote for the first time since 1962, alongside ongoing political uncertainty in South Korea and some hawkish-leaning comments from Powell," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"But despite everything that’s happening, risk assets have been broadly unphased by the various developments," Allen added.

data.title: Mesa Air Group announces increased utilization from fourth calendar quarter 2024 to first calendar quarter 2025
data.description:
• Mesa Air Group (NASDAQ:MESA) on Thursday said it will significantly increase daily aircraft utilization rates beginning in January 2025.
  • Mesa said it is scheduled to increase utilization from an average of 8.9 block hours per day in the fourth calendar quarter of 2024 to an average of 9.5 block hours per day, or a 7% increase in the first calendar quarter of 2025.
  • Utilization is planned to reach 9.8 block hours per day by March 2025, or a 10% increase from the fourth calendar quarter of 2024.
  • Further improvement in aircraft utilization is anticipated in the second calendar quarter of 2025, it said.
  • "While still below pre-Covid levels, the increase in utilization is a result of significantly reduced attrition throughout all work groups, more optimal scheduling practices, and improved operational performance," the company added.
  • MESA is +5% to $1.05.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ETFs with the largest exposure to France as it faces political turmoil
data.description:
A no-confidence vote by the French parliament at the National Assembly ousted French Prime Minister Michel Barnier, leaving the government in turmoil.

The motion had 331 votes and forced Barnier to step down after just three months in office due to the rejection by the far-right opposition of a government budget proposal he tried to pass. Now, President Emmanuel Macron is expected to appoint a new prime minister.

Investors are also concerned about a possible government collapse in the country. Some European markets have dropped amid the uncertainty.

Here are other ETFs – aside from the proprietary France ETFs, such as iShares MSCI France (NYSEARCA:EWQ) – that have exposure to French equities. Credits to ETF Database.

  • SPDR Euro Stoxx 50 ETF (FEZ) – Exposure: 33.50%

data.title: Waymo to expand services to Miami with Uber-backed Moove
data.description:
Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) autonomous vehicle driving unit Waymo is expanding its operations into Miami, the company said in a blog post Thursday.

Through its new fleet partnership with Moove, Waymo expects to work with them first in Phoenix and put them in charge for managing fleet operations, facilities, and charging infrastructure.

Waymo said it will continue to be responsible for driver validation and operation and offer the service through its existing app.

"We are excited to partner with Moove in Phoenix and later Miami, bringing together their mobility-focused fleet management experience with our growing Waymo One service," Waymo's VP of operations Ryan McNamara said.

Moove is a mobility fintech company that helps with the financing of vehicle ownership. The company has a global partnership with Uber (UBER), which led a $100 million series B funding round for the African firm. It has operations in 12 markets across Africa, the Middle East, Europe, Asia, and Latin America.

Uber and rival ride-hailing service firm Lyft (LYFT) are down 5% reacting to the announcement of the news.

data.title: Interpublic sells digital company Huge
data.description:
• Advertising and marketing solutions provider Interpublic Group (NYSE:IPG) has sold the digital company Huge to AEA Investors, a private investment firm.
  • In connection with the transaction, Huge will partner with AEA's existing portfolio company, Hero Digital.
  • The terms of the deal were not disclosed.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Apple set to invest $1B in manufacturing plant in Indonesia, investment minister says
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is looking to invest $1 billion in a manufacturing plant in Indonesia, the country's investment minister Rosan Roeslani told reporters.

The news comes a couple of days after Indonesia reportedly received an increased investment offer of $1 billion from the tech giant, as the latter looks to reverse the ban on iPhone 16 sales in the country.

In October, Indonesia had restricted Apple (AAPL) from selling its new iPhone 16 series in the country, noting that the company has yet to meet local investment requirements.

Roeslani added the details of the investment are still being finalized, the report said.

The iPhone-marker had previously proposed to invest around $100M over two years in Indonesia. Prior to that, the firm had offered to invest around $10M in Indonesia, which included investment in a factory in Bandung that makes accessories and components.

The agreement could be finalized within one week, Roeslani said.

data.title: Algorhythm Holdings shares tumble on pricing $9.5M public offering
data.description:
• Algorhythm Holdings (NASDAQ:RIME) has priced a public offering with gross proceeds of ~$9.5M.
  • The offering comprises 55.88M shares of the company’s common stock.
  • Each share of common stock or pre-funded warrant will be sold with one Series A Warrant to purchase one share of common stock at an exercise price of $0.17 per share and one Series B Warrant to purchase one share of common stock at an exercise price of $0.34 per share.
  • The Series A Warrants will expire on the five-year anniversary of its initial exercise date, and the Series B Warrants will expire on the two and one-half-year anniversary of its initial exercise date.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering for working capital and other general corporate purposes, and for repayment of certain outstanding senior secured notes of the company.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 6, 2024.
  • RIME shares fell over 45% on Thursday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mali issues arrest warrant for Barrick Gold CEO Bristow - Reuters
data.description:
Mali's government has issued an arrest warrant for Barrick Gold (NYSE:GOLD) CEO Mark Bristow, Reuters reported Thursday, in the latest escalation of tensions after four senior local employees of the company were arrested in September.

Last week, four additional employees of Barrick's (GOLD) Loulo-Gounkoto mining complex in Mali were charged and detained pending trial.

Tensions between Mali, one of Africa's biggest gold producers, and miners operating in the country have surged after the country revised its mining code with the aim of obtaining more revenues from the companies.

Bristow is accused of money laundering and violation of financial regulations, according to the warrant document dated December 2 which was seen by Reuters.

data.title: Eventbrite announces chief operating officer Lanny Baker to depart company
data.description:
• Eventbrite (NYSE:EB) announced that chief operating officer Lanny Baker plans to depart from the company, effective December 31, 2024.
  • The company also reaffirmed its financial outlook for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024.
  • EB shares were down 2.2% on Thursday.
  • Source: Press Release
